content: Tool-based memory operations can turn an AI agent's fixed working memory capacity limit into a controllable, curiosity-aligned feature: instead of forcing the agent to store all novel observations in constrained native memory, it can offload unexplored, high-curiosity patterns to external tool-managed memory for asynchronous active learning, rather than discarding them when native capacity is exceeded. This creates a scalable 'curiosity buffer' that adapts memory usage to the agent's exploration drive rather than hard architectural limits.
